reasoning: Hacking the internal partition wall between the converted-balcony study and the living room is a demolition/alteration of an internal (likely non-structural) partition wall, which HDB permits only after obtaining prior written approval. A permit is required before works start, and if any RC structural elements are affected a PE must supervise.

citations.quote: Demolition (partial or complete) or alteration of internal non-structure lightweight partition walls, such as hollow block/ brick walls, Drywalls or Ferrolite walls
All demolition or alteration of walls can only be carried out after obtaining HDB’s approval

reasoning: Removing a reinforced concrete column is removal of a load-bearing structural member, which is explicitly listed by HDB as a disallowed renovation work because it can compromise the structural integrity and safety of the building.

citations.quote: Hacking and removal of structural members such as reinforced concrete wall, columns, beams, slabs, and staircases within executive maisonettes, etc.

reasoning: No permit is required to fix shelving to a household shelter wall, but the work is only allowed under strict dimensional and material conditions: drilling must use non-metallic inserts and screws no more than 50mm deep, and any such fixtures must be removable within 48 hours upon HDB's notification for wartime shelter preparation.

citations.quote: Drilling into internal face of the HS walls and ceiling slab to a depth of not more than 50mm to affix non-metallic inserts and removable screws is allowed
Fixtures such as pictures, posters, cabinets or shelves on internal face of HS will have to be removed by flat owners within 48 hours upon notification

reasoning: Raising the bathroom floor by 80mm with cement screed exceeds the maximum allowed floor build-up thickness of 50mm (inclusive of finishes), which HDB explicitly lists as a disallowed renovation work.

citations.quote: Raising of floor level exceeding the allowed thickness of 50mm (inclusive of floor finishes) using concrete
Total thickness of floor finishes and screed must not exceed 50mm

reasoning: Installing a false ceiling (with cove lighting) in the living room does not require an HDB permit, but it is subject to conditions: it must use non-combustible materials and maintain a minimum clearance of 2.4 metres from the finished floor level. Note this applies to a suspended false ceiling system, not wet plastering directly onto the RC ceiling slab, which is separately disallowed.

citations.quote: Non-combustible materials must be used for the false ceilings, cornices, pelmet and decorative panels
the minimum clearance height of 2.4 metres measured from finished floor level

reasoning: Repainting bedroom walls (spray painting) and replacing an internal bedroom door with an ornamental timber/plastic door are both listed as not requiring an HDB permit, with no dimensional or PE conditions attached beyond using pre-packed plastering material if plastering is involved.

citations.quote: Wallpaper, cement and sand plastering, gypsum plastering, rock-stone finish, spray painting
Replacement of existing internal doors with ornamental timber/ plastic doors
